title: Symmetries of hamiltonian actions of reductive groups
speaker: David Ben-Zvi
abstract: Classical and quantum Hamiltonian actions of reductive groups, respectively, give rise to ubiquitous families of commuting flows and of commutative rings of operators. I will explain how a construction of Ngô (from the proof of the Fundamental Lemma) provides a universal integration of these flows for classical systems. I will then explain, following joint work with Sam Gunningham, how to quantize the Ngô action to obtain universal symmetries of the corresponding quantum systems.

proof_stages.label: Classical and quantum Hamiltonian G-actions
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes a characteristic-zero setting with a reductive group G and formulates classical moment maps and quantum moment maps as actions of the symplectic or quantum groupoid attached to G.

proof_stages.label: Commuting Hamiltonians from invariants and centers
proof_stages.summary: Composing a classical moment map with the adjoint quotient, or restricting a quantum moment map U(g)->A to Z(U(g)), produces G-invariant pairwise commuting flows or differential operators that descend to Hamiltonian reductions and define Harish-Chandra eigensystems.

proof_stages.label: Classical integration by regular centralizers
proof_stages.summary: The classical integration problem is formulated as finding a flat commutative group scheme over the invariant-polynomial base, and Ngô's group scheme J of regular centralizers with its canonical map to ordinary centralizers is presented as the universal solution.

proof_stages.label: Satake reconstruction of the Ngô action
proof_stages.summary: A new structural construction uses geometric Satake as a nonabelian Fourier transform, translating functions or sheaves on J into affine-Grassmannian convolution data so that the interaction of tensor product and convolution yields the Ngô action without Hartogs extension.

proof_stages.label: Quantum integration as symmetry of eigensystems
proof_stages.summary: Quantizing integration is reinterpreted as capturing intertwiners among Harish-Chandra eigensystems, leading from eigenvalues in h^*/W to additional lattice-translation symmetries encoded by the affine Weyl group.

proof_stages.label: Universal Whittaker quantum groupoid
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts the existence of a commutative Hopf algebroid WH over Z(U(g)), described both by Whittaker differential operators and by equivariant homology of the affine Grassmannian, which acts on every quantum Hamiltonian G-space and has classical limit J.

proof_stages.label: Categorical form and Langlands-parameter consequence
proof_stages.summary: Passing to modules, WH gives a symmetric monoidal category identified with sheaves on h^*/W_aff that maps centrally to sheaves on G and hence acts on all G-categories or reductions, yielding a toy local geometric-Langlands parameterization.
